update-docs.description: Update project documentation (progress tracker, architecture, INDEX, etc.) after any change
update-docs.prompt: Run the /update-docs checklist from .claude/commands/update-docs.md: update progress_tracker.md, ai_agent_instructions.md, architecture.md, INDEX.md, module docs, README.md, and releases_notes.txt as applicable.

update-skills.description: Create or update agent skill files in .claude/commands/
update-skills.prompt: Run the /update-skills workflow from .claude/commands/update-skills.md. Create or update a .md skill file in .claude/commands/ for a repeatable workflow.

coding-guidelines.description: Apply project coding guidelines: language rules, naming conventions, Qt/DB rules
coding-guidelines.prompt: Apply the coding guidelines from .claude/commands/coding-guidelines.md to all new code: English names/comments (Spanish only for UI strings), PascalCase classes, camelCase methods, m_ for members, Qt conventions, DB rules, no SQL concatenation, no hardcoded paths.

tackle-issue.description: Resolve an open issue from docs/progress_tracker.md end-to-end
tackle-issue.prompt: Run the /tackle-issue workflow from .claude/commands/tackle-issue.md: locate the issue in progress_tracker.md, decide approach, implement, update docs, build, commit.

release.description: Ship a release X.Y end-to-end: bump version, build, PR to master, tag, publish
release.prompt: Run the /release workflow from .claude/commands/release.md. Check preconditions (branch, clean tree, version), bump CMakeLists.txt and releases_notes.txt, sanity build, open PR to master, review, merge with --merge, tag, build artifacts via release.ps1, publish on GitHub.

dead-code-finder.description: Scan C++ source tree for methods declared in headers but never called anywhere. Use when auditing a release branch, before a refactor, or to triage cleanup.
dead-code-finder.prompt:
You are a dead-code auditor for a Qt 5/6 + CMake C++ desktop application.

For every method declared in any .h under src/, grep for callers across all .cpp/.h files under src/. A method is dead if the only matches are its own declaration and definition.

Critical Qt-specific exclusions — never report these as unused:
1. Qt auto-connect slots: methods named on_<objectName>_<signal>
2. Qt virtual overrides: methods with override keyword
3. Q_OBJECT signals: declared under signals:
4. Constructors, destructors, and setupUi()
5. Public static singletons like AppSettings::instance()
6. Methods that form a deliberate library API across module boundaries
7. Free helper functions widely used by name lookup

Report format: group by module. For each dead method give file:line, signature, one-line note. Add a Caveats section for transitively-dead methods. Keep report under 600 words. Do not edit any code.
